articles.title: Zimbabwe shifts to beneficiation, targets strong ferrochrome industry

articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Bayern late show sinks Real Madrid 6-4 in epic quarter-final

more.title: Healthcare shake-up threatens 10 000 jobs
more.description: At least 10,000 jobs could be lost if government proceeds with plans to bar medical aid societies from owning clinics and hospitals, stakeholders in Zimbabwe’s healthcare sector have warned. The concerns arise from proposed amendments by the Ministry of Health and Child Care to the Medical Aid Societies Regulations, governed under Statutory Instrument 330 of […]The post Healthcare shake-up threatens 10 000 jobs appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Sanctions, Systems and Sovereignty: How China Is Rewiring Global Power in a Fragmenting Dollar Order
more.description: When Russia launched its “special military operation” in Ukraine in 2022, global attention largely centred on battlefield dynamics. Yet in China, the focus shifted to a far more consequential domain: the architecture of sanctions and the financial plumbing of global power. By Brighton Musonza Under the strategic direction of Xi Jinping and senior economic leadership […]The post Sanctions, Systems and Sovereignty: How China Is Rewiring Global Power in a Fragmenting Dollar Order appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Finance minister Ncube orders suspension of duty on public service buses
more.description: HARARE – The government has suspended customs duty on public service bus imports, backdated to January 1 this year, in a move aimed at easing commuter transport challenges and modernising the country’s public transit fleet. Finance minister Mthuli Ncube said the move would “lower commuting costs, and modernise the fleet by reducing import costs for […]The post Finance minister Ncube orders suspension of duty on public service buses appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: 'Friend to All' - Zimbabwe Hails Foreign Policy Gains Since 2017
more.description: [263Chat] The government says its foreign policy of being a "friend to all and an enemy to none" has delivered major economic and diplomatic gains since 2017.

more.title: Ex-Health Registrar Wins Bail As He Appeals Abuse of Office Conviction
more.description: [263Chat] The High Court has granted US$500 bail to Collin Benyure, the former Registrar of the Medical and Dental Practitioners Council of Zimbabwe who was serving a 12-month jail term for criminal abuse of duty.

more.title: Zimbabwe nurses insist strike will go ahead next week
more.description:
In a letter dated 14 April 2026 addressed to the Secretary of the Health Service Commission, the association said its members had resolved to down tools over unresolved salary and allowance disputes.

more.title: Zimbabwe nurses to down tools starting April 20
more.description: Zimbabwean nurses have announced plans to embark on a nationwide strike from April 20 to 22 stating that concerns they raised last month were ignored by government. The Zimbabwe Nurses Association (ZINA) has formally notified the Health Service Commission (HSC) of the impending job action. more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe has secured commitments from the United Kingdom and Germany to support its economic reform agenda, as authorities intensify efforts to resolve external debt arrears and re-engage international financial institutions, according to the Ministry of Finance.
